phases.name: philosophy list surfaces each seed's aliases (alias discoverability)

steps.name: render the aliases line in each philosophy list block
steps.description: slice-30 made `philosophy show <alias>` resolve, but `philosophy list` never shows WHICH alias strings a philosophy carries, so the shorthand is undiscoverable. Append an `aliases: <joined>` line to each list block in render_philosophy_list (crates/cli/src/render/philosophy.rs), rendered only when the seed has aliases. Bare comma-separated alias strings (mirroring render_record's convention) — NOT alias object-ids — so the slice-22 distinct-object-id count (PV-2) and object-id-form checks (PV-3) are untouched.
criteria: OWNED every_seed_renders_its_aliases_in_the_list (each seed's exact `aliases: <joined>` line renders; a no-alias seed renders no empty label)
ACCEPTANCE philosophy_list_surfaces_seed_aliases (CLI `philosophy list` stdout carries an `aliases:` label and the unambiguous `mem-safety` alias)
GUARDRAIL slice-22 philosophy_vocabulary acceptance (PV-1..PV-6: object ids, >=10 distinct count, id form, --json, text-default, network-disabled) stays green
steps.implementation_notes:
render_seed_block appends `
  aliases: {joined}` only when !seed.aliases.is_empty(); blocks stay 

-separated (the join is unchanged). Aliases render as bare strings, never NSID-prefixed object-ids, so distinct_object_ids (PV-2) does not count them and PV-3's object-id-form guard is unaffected. --json (PV-4) already serializes aliases via the full record — unchanged. Pure renderer change; no crate/dep/route change, check-arch stays 21. Demo: openlore philosophy list.

validation.review_verdict: APPROVED — single-step pure-renderer polish. RED demonstrated before GREEN; 1 owned pure pin + 1 acceptance scenario; slice-22 list contract (object-id count/form, json, text-default) preserved by rendering bare aliases only.
